args_redacted.content:
Linux Network Diagnostics Tools - Summary
===========================================

Based on research, here are the standard Linux network diagnostic tools:

1. ping - Tests basic connectivity to a remote host (ICMP echo)
2. traceroute / tracepath - Shows the route packets take to a destination
3. ss - Socket statistics (modern replacement for netstat)
4. netstat - Network statistics and connections
5. ip - Modern tool for network interface and routing configuration
6. nslookup / dig - DNS lookup tools
7. curl / wget - HTTP/HTTPS connectivity testing
8. nc (netcat) - TCP/UDP port testing
9. nmap - Network port scanning
10. mtr - Combines ping and traceroute
11. tcpdump - Packet capture and analysis
12. ethtool - Network interface configuration and diagnostics

Standard Connectivity Test Commands:
- ping -c 4 8.8.8.8          (test basic network reachability)
- ping -c 4 google.com       (test DNS + network reachability)
- traceroute google.com      (trace network path)
- ss -tuln                   (list listening ports)
- ip addr show               (show network interfaces)
- curl -I https://google.com (test HTTP connectivity)
- nslookup google.com        (test DNS resolution)

Note: Shell execution was restricted in this environment, so actual commands could not be run. The above is a reference guide of standard Linux network diagnostics tools and commands.
